transformation_type: vicinal diol oxidative cleavage to carbonyl fragments
mechanism_type: lead tetraacetate-mediated glycol cleavage via cyclic lead intermediates
common_conditions: treat vicinal diols with lead tetraacetate under anhydrous conditions to cleave the C-C bond and form carbonyl compounds
description_short: Oxidative cleavage of vicinal diols to carbonyl compounds using lead tetraacetate, often as a mild alternative for advanced cyclic diol fragmentation.

transformation_type: carboxylic acid derivative to isocyanate then amine / urea / carbamate
mechanism_type: thermal acyl azide rearrangement with nitrogen extrusion to isocyanate
common_conditions: generate acyl azide from acid derivative, heat to rearrange to isocyanate, then trap with water, amine, or alcohol
description_short: Thermal rearrangement of acyl azides to isocyanates, enabling conversion of carboxylic acids into amines, ureas, and carbamates.

transformation_type: activated aromatic aldehyde or ketone to substituted phenol
mechanism_type: hydroperoxide addition followed by 1,2-aryl shift and hydrolysis of O-acylphenol
common_conditions: treat activated aromatic aldehydes or ketones with hydrogen peroxide or peracid, then hydrolyze the O-acylphenol intermediate to phenol
description_short: Oxidation/rearrangement of activated aromatic aldehydes or ketones to substituted phenols through peroxide addition and aryl migration.

mechanism_type: anhydride-mediated N-acylation, oxazolone formation, decarboxylative acyl transfer
common_conditions: treat alpha-amino acids with anhydride and base to form mixed anhydride/oxazolone intermediates that collapse to alpha-acylamino ketones
description_short: Transformation of alpha-amino acids into alpha-acylamino ketones under anhydride/base conditions through mixed anhydride and oxazolone intermediates.

transformation_type: cyclobutenone or diazoketone annulation with activated alkyne to substituted arene
mechanism_type: vinylketene generation followed by cycloaddition and electrocyclic rearrangement
common_conditions: heat cyclobutenones or generate vinylketenes from diazoketones in the presence of activated heterosubstituted alkynes to forge substituted aromatic rings
description_short: Annulation method that converts cyclobutenone- or diazoketone-derived vinylketenes and activated alkynes into densely substituted aromatic rings.
